transcript_facts:
* The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
* The patient denies blood in stool.
* Hemoglobin is 8.2.
* The clinician plans an anemia profile.
* The clinician starts Lasix 40 mg daily.
* The patient reports sleeping well at night.
* The patient reports no thoughts of hurting herself.
* The patient reports high blood pressure in the past.
* The patient reports checking her blood pressure.
* The patient’s blood pressure has been up and down lately.
* The patient has been eating a lot of frozen meals.
* The patient reports headaches.
* The patient reports numbness in her hands.
* The patient reports tingling in her fingers.
* The clinician’s exam reveals clear lungs.
* The clinician’s exam reveals no adenopathy in the neck.
* The clinician’s exam reveals no hyperthyroidism.
* The clinician’s exam reveals a regular heart rhythm without murmurs.
* The clinician’s exam reveals equal grips.
* The clinician’s exam reveals neurological intactness.
* The clinician’s exam reveals good strength.
* The clinician’s exam reveals good pulses.
* The clinician’s exam is essentially normal.
* The patient’s blood pressure is elevated in the office today.
* The clinician plans to increase lisinopril to 10 milligrams a day.
* The clinician plans to follow up in one month.
* The clinician does not want to change any other medications at this time.
* The patient’s carpal tunnel release is doing well.
